title: Fibonacci Sequence Generator with Incorrect Handling of Zero Input

statement.markdown: Write a function that generates the Fibonacci sequence up to the nth number. The function should take one argument n, where n is the number of Fibonacci numbers to generate. For example, if n = 5, the function should return [0, 1, 1, 2, 3]. If n is less than 1, the function should return an empty list. If n is 1, the function should return a list containing only the first Fibonacci number, 0.
statement.inputFormat: An integer n, where n is the number of Fibonacci numbers to generate.
statement.outputFormat: A list of integers representing the Fibonacci sequence.
statement.notes: You should handle the edge cases of n being less than 1.

constraints.description: The number of Fibonacci numbers to generate.

examples.explanation: If n = 5, the function should return [0, 1, 1, 2, 3].
If n is 1, the function should return a list containing only the first Fibonacci number, 0.
If n is less than 1, the function should return an empty list.

hints.text: First hint text: Make sure to handle the edge case where n is less than 1.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Use a loop to generate the Fibonacci sequence.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Generate the Fibonacci sequence by iterating over the range from 0 to n.
editorial.betterSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a loop to generate the Fibonacci sequence, starting with the first two numbers, 0 and 1.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a loop to generate the Fibonacci sequence, starting with the first two numbers, 0 and 1.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The generated sequence will be the correct Fibonacci sequence.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Generate the Fibonacci sequence for n = 5.
